quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika, the country's most famous spice, comes in eight different varieties ranging from delicate and mild to fiery hot. The town of Szeged is so famous for its paprika that it has an entire museum dedicated to the beloved red spice!

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in Puget Sound
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals, those curious faces you might spot in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, are remarkable divers who can hold their breath for nearly 30 minutes. Learn about their fascinating behaviors and how scientists track their underwater journeys.

medium_read.title: Olympic National Park's Rainforest Magic
medium_read.teaser: The Hoh Rainforest, just a drive from Port Angeles, receives up to 14 feet of rain annually, creating a mystical green world unlike anywhere else in the continental United States. Discover what makes these ancient forests so special and the incredible creatures that call them home.

long_read.title: The Remarkable Journey of Hungarian Immigrants to America
long_read.teaser: Between 1880 and 1920, nearly two million Hungarians made the brave journey to America, bringing their rich traditions, foods, and culture. This touching exploration shares their stories of courage, community, and the lasting legacy they created in their new homeland.

port_angeles.title: Bald Eagles at Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: Ediz Hook, the natural sand spit that protects Port Angeles Harbor, is one of the best spots to observe bald eagles year-round. These majestic birds often perch on driftwood logs or soar above the water hunting for fish. Early morning and late afternoon offer the best viewing opportunities!

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's exactly right, Barbara! What an incredible little bird with such a powerful heart!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — amazing for such a tiny creature!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: A hummingbird's tiny heart actually beats about 1,200 times per minute while flying — that's 20 times per second! Just remarkable.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To support their rapid metabolism and constant hovering, hummingbirds have the fastest heart rate of all birds. They also consume about half their body weight in sugar daily from flower nectar!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's right, Barbara — wonderful to keep these beautiful words close to your heart!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word for 'thank you' is 'köszönöm' (or 'köszi' informally). Such a lovely part of your Hungarian heritage, Ildiko!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is unique among European languages — it's part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it more closely related to Finnish and Estonian than to neighboring Slavic or Germanic languages. Its beautiful sounds and expressions carry centuries of Magyar culture.

puzzle_3.question: What percentage of an iceberg's mass is typically hidden below the water's surface?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! About 90% is hidden below — that's where the phrase 'tip of the iceberg' comes from. You've got it!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Actually, about 90% of an iceberg is hidden beneath the waves! That's why they were so dangerous to ships and why we say 'tip of the iceberg' when we're only seeing a small part of something.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Ice is slightly less dense than seawater, causing icebergs to float with most of their mass submerged. The beautiful blue color you sometimes see in icebergs comes from ice so compressed that it absorbs all colors of light except blue.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
